message:
refactor(fcp-cbor): drop unused canonicalize_map_scratch_capacity forwarder + migrate tests to the _with_limit form

Follow-up cleanup on the `fix(fcp-cbor): enforce per-key byte
budget in canonicalize_map` + `refactor(fcp-cbor): simplify
canonicalize_map capped-writer error mapping` pair. After both
commits landed, the thin forwarder

    fn canonicalize_map_scratch_capacity(entry_count: usize) -> usize {
        canonicalize_map_scratch_capacity_with_limit(entry_count, MAX_CANONICAL_OBJECT_BYTES)
    }

had no remaining production callers — every site that previously
used it had been updated to call
`canonicalize_map_scratch_capacity_with_limit(entry_count,
byte_limit)` directly with the byte_limit it was carrying anyway.
The only surviving references were test sites in `mod tests`.

This commit:

1. Removes the `canonicalize_map_scratch_capacity` forwarder
   function. Dead-code elimination; clippy's
   `unused_fn` would have caught it on the next lint pass.

2. Migrates the two test assertions in
   `canonicalize_map_scratch_capacity_hits_exact_cap_boundary` to
   the `_with_limit` form by passing `MAX_CANONICAL_OBJECT_BYTES`
   explicitly as the second argument. Tests still prove the same
   property (the scratch cap saturates at exactly
   `MAX_CANONICAL_OBJECT_BYTES` when `entry_count *
   32 >= MAX_CANONICAL_OBJECT_BYTES`), just through the now-
   canonical API.

No behavior change; removal is internal, and the test migration
is cosmetic — assertions pass the same values through to the
same helper. Clippy surfaces stays clean after this.
